key_points: Hillside dorms are popular and have good memories for grads
Triple rooms existed but were recently converted to doubles
Room layouts are fairly standard; students ask about customization options
key_quotes: They changed the whole triple dorm thing to a double instead this year so thank god for that
I lived in hillside my freshman year as well, this brought back good memories!
Are all of the rooms in hillside like this? Like can I have this layout in all of the rooms?

key_points: URI has a party school reputation; students actively ask if it's a 'fun party school'
Greek life appears central to social connections — hard to make friends without it
Campus culture described as very liberal/progressive with active identity-centered programming
key_quotes: I read some reviews that it's hard to make friends at URI if you aren't a part of greek life. Is this true?
It's an extremely woke, liberal university. They legit have a place called the Gender & Sexuality Center for Sex Toy Bingo
Is it a fun party school? I am thinking about going to Alabama as well

key_points: No direct dining feedback found in available data
Campus life broadly remembered fondly by alumni but dining not specifically praised
Insufficient data to score accurately — worth researching independently
key_quotes: By far the best four years of my life! College is so much more than classes and partying.
Enjoy it while you can

key_points: Engineering students openly discuss constant workload and burnout risk
Students warn incoming freshmen not to flunk out even if they hate their major
Stress management is a real concern with part-time jobs on top of coursework
key_quotes: I'm also an engineering student and I'm always working plus I also have a part time job and life is soo tiresome… how do you cope with the stress/burnout?
Don't flunk out of classes even if you hate your major. It really negatively affects your GPA.
When you said you basically are always working, I felt that!!

key_points: URI publishes merit scholarship amounts based on stats — transparency is a plus
URI cost cited at ~$33k/year, cheaper than comparable alternatives like Wentworth ($40k)
Gilman scholarship emails sent to students but require full applications — not guaranteed
key_quotes: URI is cheaper for me ($33k for URI and $40k for Wentworth)
You need to apply for it. It's probably a mass email they send out to everyone.
University of Rhode Island [publishes merit scholarship amounts based on your stats]

key_points: Engineering programs are intense and demanding — students confirm constant workload
One alumnus transferred OUT of URI's electrical engineering program in the 80s calling it a 'nightmare'
Pharmacy, environmental science, and oceanography are frequently cited as strong programs
key_quotes: Went there in the mid 80s~ what a nightmare at electrical engineering. I transferred to The University Of Hawaii @ Manoa because thats as far as I could get from URI & still speak English.
I recently started my journey as an engineering student and when you said you basically are always working, I felt that!!
The most competitive school i got into was University of Rhode Island, i was just applying to apply lol

key_points: Gilman scholarship mass emails mislead students into thinking they're selected — they're not
Website information on graduate programs is unclear and hard to navigate
Limited direct complaints about administration but transparency issues flagged
key_quotes: Potential candidate or you got selected? Those are wildly different.
You still need to apply. Not to burst your bubble, but it's probably a mass email they send out to everyone.
Their website boasts a great MS program, but it never explicitly states that it is an accelerated program

key_points: Close to both Boston (2 hrs) and NYC (3 hrs) — huge advantage for travel and internships
Narragansett Beach nearby; campus nostalgia is strong among alumni
Rhode Island's small size means the location punches above its weight for access
key_quotes: I vote for University of Rhode Island - mostly because it's relatively close to both Boston (two hours) and New York (three hours).
We actually took a ride down to Narragansett Beach today with our dogs and reminisced about the good ol' days at URI.
I graduated four years ago from URI... I also met my wife there! By far the best four years of my life!

key_points: Incoming freshman already asking about internships vs. Greek life — career awareness is present
Engineering students motivated by career outcomes but burnout is a real barrier
No strong data on post-grad placement rates or salary outcomes from this dataset
key_quotes: I'll be a freshman at URI this year and I've been caught in a conundrum. Do I try and rush for a frat or try to apply for internships?
As an incoming freshman, I would focus on getting adjusted to college life. There will be plenty of time for Greek life and internships later.
Can you pls tell which are the best internships for chemical engineering to get job easier

key_points: At ~$33k/year, URI is meaningfully cheaper than some comparable engineering schools
Published merit scholarship structure gives applicants clear expectations upfront
CCRI students using the 2-year transfer pipeline to URI to cut costs — a real strategy
key_quotes: URI is cheaper for me ($33k for URI and $40k for Wentworth)
lol im at CCRI right now for mechanical engineering and plan on transferring to URI after the free two years
University of Rhode Island [publishes what stats get you what scholarship]

key_points: Alumni consistently look back on URI with strong nostalgia and warmth
Party school reputation is real but engineering culture shows serious academic side too
Students describe it as accessible and fun — not elite, but genuinely enjoyable
key_quotes: By far the best four years of my life! Enjoy every second of it, because it goes by too fast.
I love this video and I am so excited to go to URI this year!
The most competitive school i got into was University of Rhode Island, i was just applying to apply lol

key_points: Social isolation: hard to make friends at URI without Greek life involvement
Academics: electrical engineering specifically called a 'nightmare' by at least one alumnus who transferred out
Administration: misleading mass Gilman emails create false impressions of selection
key_quotes: I read some reviews that it's hard to make friends at URI if you aren't a part of greek life. Is this true?
Went there in the mid 80s~ what a nightmare at electrical engineering. I transferred to The University Of Hawaii @ Manoa because thats as far as I could get from URI & still speak English.
Not to burst your bubble, but it's probably a mass email they send out to everyone.

key_points: Gilman scholarship program actively recruits URI students for international study
Proximity to Narragansett Beach makes the location genuinely special for outdoor life
CCRI-to-URI transfer pipeline is a smart, low-cost path for Rhode Island engineering students
key_quotes: I go to the University of Rhode Island and today I received an email saying I was a potential candidate for the Gilman scholarship for studying and interning abroad
We actually took a ride down to Narragansett Beach today with our dogs and reminisced about the good ol' days at URI.
